title: Borrowing Against Gold
scenario: A limited-edition phone you want costs ₹70,000 and you are ₹40,000 short. A lender offers a gold loan against your mother's bangles at around 14% interest. The gadget is a pure want, not a need, and the gold is family jewellery.

choices.label: Skip the loan, save up for two more months, and buy it in cash
choices.lesson: Borrowing for a discretionary want means paying interest for something you could simply wait and save for.

choices.label: Take the gold loan now so you do not miss the launch offer
choices.lesson: Pledging family gold for a want puts a meaningful asset at risk of forfeiture and adds interest cost to a depreciating gadget.
